summary: Exact-match decompilation PR touching IF textlib, MN count/event/main, and TY display/list code, plus a TY header cleanup. The PR body claims 6 file(s) and 8 newly matched functions with clean isolated and full-ship verification; changed-file metadata also includes toy.h. Review centered on avoiding data/extern-driven regressions, checking GET_JOBJ versus a local inline, and inlining TU-owned float literals.

key_files.role: Matched/improved IF text library functions, including un_80303444 and un_80304D30; introduced small inline helpers and a narrow PAD_STACK in un_80304D30.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: un_80303444 direct arg0->x8 indexing and typed q casts; un_80304D30_idk/un_80304D30_48C0/un_80304D30_4B0C inline helpers; PAD_STACK(4)
text_corpus issue_comment report: main/melee/if/textlib un_80303444 99.15% -> 100.00%; un_80304D30 94.35% -> 99.96%

key_files.role: Matched/improved MN count code and data; added AnimLoopSettings data, an inline combined VS play time accessor, stack/lifetime shaping around row creation, and changed a displayed fallback string.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: mnCount_803EFA88 array; mnCount_GetCombinedVSPlayTimeValue; volatile value_text block; buf1 scoped inside time branch
review_comment r3399295198: PsiLupan flagged static char lbl_803EFB60 string access as a regression
text_corpus issue_comment report: mnCount_GetRowValue_Number 95.98% -> 100.00%; mnCount_CreateRow 96.51% -> 97.32%; .data and .sdata improvements

key_files.role: Improved event menu functions by reordering JObj lookups, introducing event_gobj/page locals, and adjusting casts for animation requests and input assignment.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: mnEvent_8024E524 adds event_gobj; fn_8024D864 adds page local and combined inputs = mn_804A04F0.buttons = mn_80229624(4)

key_files.role: Improved main menu function fn_8022AFEC through scoped temporaries and repeated cur_menu locals around selection change calls.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: tmp_data block around gp->user_data; cur_menu scoped before mn_80229F60 and mn_8022A440

key_files.role: Header cleanup removing extern float declarations tied to TY literal/data handling.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: removed extern float un_804DDE60, un_804DDE4C, un_804DDE48

key_files.role: Improved TY display functions, simplified control flow, used an inline helper for JObj access after GET_JOBJ was tested and reported to worsen the match, and had review concerns about extern/data-order matching.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: un_8031B328 assignment-in-if for HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress; simplified un_804D6F20 condition; un_8031BF34_inline returns (HSD_JObj*) un_804D6F2C->hsd_obj
review_comment r3399300105: PsiLupan said matching because of externs is not correct and indicates unfinished data ordering
review_comment r3404481894: jurrejelle asked whether GET_JOBJ could be used
review_comment r3404742756: author reported GET_JOBJ(un_804D6F2C) drops un_8031BF34 from 98.17% to 97.09% fuzzy due to arg evaluation/codegen and kept inline helper

key_files.role: Largest TY list change; replaced many extern float accesses with inline literals, adjusted stack padding and HSD_Text temporary use, and achieved .sdata2 match per bot report.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: removed extern f32 un_804DDE28/un_804DDE2C/un_804DDE30/un_804DDE34/un_804DDE38/un_804DDE3C/un_804DDE40/un_804DDE44/un_804DDE4C/un_804DDE50; literals 6.5f, 0.41f, 14.7f, 0.028f, 0.029f; PAD_STACK(0x60)
review_comment r3404485635: jurrejelle suggested inlining likely float constants
review_comment r3404742909: author said they were this TU's own .sdata2 literal pool and inlined 0.028f/0.029f byte-exact as 3ce56042/3ced9168 with unchanged function match state
text_corpus issue_comment report: main/melee/ty/tylist .sdata2 37.84% -> 100.00%; un_803148E4 92.59% -> 100.00%; un_80312904 87.96% -> 99.06%

what_changed: PR body reports exact-match decompilation of 6 source files with 8 newly matched functions and clean regression verification against baseline 0b15e71345.
Changed-file metadata lists 7 touched paths: six C files plus src/melee/ty/toy.h.
Bot report recorded 5 new matches and 12 improvements, including full matches for tylist .sdata2, tylist un_803148E4, mncount mnCount_GetRowValue_Number, textlib un_80303444, and mncount .sdata.
TY list float extern usage was reduced by inlining literals; toy.h removed three extern float declarations.
Review discussion led to explicit evidence that GET_JOBJ(un_804D6F2C) was tried for un_8031BF34 but worsened fuzzy score, so a no-arg inline helper was retained.
Review discussion identified string/extern/data-order matching concerns in mncount.c and tydisplay.c.

smart_moves.text: The author tested a reviewer-suggested GET_JOBJ cleanup and reported the exact local fuzzy-score regression before keeping the inline helper.
The author responded to float-literal review with TU ownership and byte-exact literal evidence for 0.028f/0.029f while noting function match state was unchanged.
The PR included narrow verification claims in the body: isolated ninja changes_all clean report and full ship-set verification with 0 regressions.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Do not convert ordinary strings into generated static buffers or access them through artificial storage just to chase matching; review treated that as a regression.
Matching through externs can be rejected as a data-order workaround; if data ordering is unfinished, avoid using extern indirection as the source-level explanation.
When small float symbols are actually a TU-owned literal pool, inlining the literals can be the cleaner source form; preserve byte-exact evidence when making that call.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: tylist.c inline 0.028f/0.029f and other float literals

decomp_lessons.lesson: GET_JOBJ should be tested rather than applied mechanically; in this PR a GET_JOBJ form changed argument evaluation/codegen and worsened the local match, while a no-argument inline preserved better matching.

naming_conventions.convention: Address-style names such as un_80304D30_idk, un_80304D30_48C0, un_80304D30_4B0C, un_8031BF34_inline, and mnCount_803EFA88 were retained or introduced where semantics were not fully proven in the slice.
Short role locals were used where roles were apparent, such as text, event_gobj, page, cur_menu, tmp_data, and value_text.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Scoped temporary locals and declaration/lifetime changes were used to steer codegen, including tmp_data, cur_menu, event_gobj, page, text, and a volatile value_text.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: mnmain.c scoped tmp_data and cur_menu blocks

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK was adjusted narrowly in functions with stack-sensitive matching: PAD_STACK(4) in textlib un_80304D30 and PAD_STACK(0x60) in tylist un_80312904.
Assignment-in-condition forms were used in tydisplay un_8031B328 for HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress results, likely as a codegen/control-flow matching shape.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: tydisplay.c lightData/fogDesc assignment inside if

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: A no-argument inline helper was retained for JObj access after the author reported GET_JOBJ changed call-site evaluation and worsened fuzzy matching.
Float externs in tylist were replaced with inline numeric literals once review and author evidence identified them as TU-owned .sdata2 literals.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: tylist.c extern removals and inline literals

review_feedback.feedback: Flagged generated/static string access as a regression: strings should not be accessed this way.
Flagged a similar regression: matching because of externs is not correct and indicates unfinished data ordering.
Asked whether GET_JOBJ could be used, while allowing the current form if it did not work.
Reported testing GET_JOBJ(un_804D6F2C), which reduced un_8031BF34 fuzzy score from 98.17% to 97.09%, so the inline helper was kept.
Suggested likely float constants should be inlined.
Confirmed the constants were this TU's .sdata2 literal pool and inlined 0.028f/0.029f with byte-exact hex evidence.

follow_up_queries: PR2658 string access regression mnCount_InitUserData_noinline lbl_803EFB60
PR2658 extern matching regression tyDisplay Table Scale Irregul

evidence_refs: diff_excerpt/raw_diff truncated but includes key hunks
notes: The raw diff is truncated in loaded context, so some tylist hunks and some review-addressing details are not visible in full.
The PR body lists 6 files, while changed-file metadata lists 7 paths because toy.h was also changed.
PsiLupan's data/string regression comments are visible, but the loaded slice does not include a direct author response to those two comments; only final PR merge and later approval are present.
Verification claims are taken from PR body and bot report; no independent build or objdiff was run during intake.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Test GET_JOBJ/header cleanup against local codegen before replacing a direct JObj cast
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2658, a reviewer suggested GET_JOBJ for un_8031BF34. The author reported that GET_JOBJ(un_804D6F2C) changed argument evaluation at the call site and dropped the fuzzy score from 98.17% to 97.09%, so a no-argument inline helper was kept.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Inline TU-owned float literals instead of preserving generated float externs
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2658, review suggested inlining likely float constants in tylist; the author identified them as the TU's own .sdata2 literal pool and inlined 0.028f/0.029f with byte-exact evidence while function match state was unchanged.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Avoid string and extern-driven data ordering workarounds in code-matching PRs
accepted_candidate_records.text: Review on PR #2658 called out generated/static string access and extern-based matching as regressions, with the extern comment explicitly saying data ordering was not finished.

source_update_candidates.title: Add PR #2658 as an example under no string-literal symbol regression / text-before-data matching
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2658 review provides concrete examples: static/generated string access in mncount was called a regression, and extern-based matching in tydisplay was rejected as evidence of unfinished data ordering.
source_update_candidates.reason: Existing standards already cover this pattern; this PR can serve as a grounded example for future reviewers and agents.

source_update_candidates.title: Add PR #2658 GET_JOBJ counterexample to header-inlines guidance
source_update_candidates.text: GET_JOBJ is not unconditional cleanup: in PR #2658, GET_JOBJ(un_804D6F2C) reportedly worsened un_8031BF34 fuzzy score from 98.17% to 97.09% because argument evaluation changed codegen, so a local no-arg inline helper was retained.
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded standards already say GET_JOBJ should only be used when local evidence supports it; this PR is a useful historical example.

source_update_candidates.title: Add PR #2658 float literal pool example to literals-and-data-ownership
source_update_candidates.text: In PR #2658 tylist, 0.028f/0.029f were identified as this TU's .sdata2 literal pool and inlined byte-exact, avoiding unnecessary extern float symbols.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: tylist.c removed extern f32 symbols and inlined literals
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a concrete example of the existing literal/data ownership standard.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all GET_JOBJ uses are bad; this PR only shows one local case where GET_JOBJ changed codegen.
Do not infer that every .sdata2 float should be inlined; this PR only supports inlining when TU literal-pool ownership and byte-exact evidence exist.
Do not treat the PR's PAD_STACK and volatile local uses as general style; standards require explicit verifier evidence and narrow scope.
